name: LG CLOi home robot
short_description: LG's family of AI home and service robots that use voice and vision to assist with domestic tasks and control connected appliances.
long_description: CLOi is LG's home-robot brand, first unveiled at CES 2018, spanning a tabletop Hub Robot assistant and mobile service robots such as the CleanBot and PortBot. The robots move and sense autonomously, respond to spoken commands, and tie into LG's ThinQ smart-home platform to run appliances. CLOi matters for physical AI in daily life because it tries to give an ambient home assistant a moving, expressive body rather than leaving it as a disembodied voice in a speaker, and its 2026 CLOiD successor pushes the same idea toward a two-armed humanoid for household chores.

images.caption: LG CLOi CleanBot, an autonomous home service robot, shown at IFA 2018 in Berlin.
images.alt_text: A white wheeled LG CLOi service robot with a rounded body and small screen face standing on a dark exhibition floor at a trade show.

sources.title: LG @ IFA 2018: your ultimate guide to LG CLOi robots
sources.access_notes: Official LG page describing the CLOi robot family shown at IFA 2018.

sources.title: LG to Unveil Home Robot at CES 2026, Sharing Vision for the "Zero Labor Home"
sources.access_notes: Press release announcing the CLOiD home robot, successor to the CLOi line.

interactions.title: Tell the room to start the wash
interactions.description: Speak to the CLOi Hub Robot and have it change a washing-machine cycle, switch on the TV, or push a recipe to the fridge — a single voice front-end for the connected ThinQ appliances around the house.

interactions.title: Walk a hotel guest to the elevators
interactions.description: The GuideBot greets an arriving guest, answers a question about the bar or local attractions, then rolls ahead at walking pace to physically escort them to the elevators.

interactions.title: Run a museum exhibit on wheels
interactions.description: Stationed in a gallery, the GuideBot plays exhibit video on its twin touchscreens, recites information aloud, and lets visitors tap through interactive content as it leads them between displays.

interactions.title: Pull the milk and start breakfast
interactions.description: On command the two-armed CLOiD opens the fridge to take out milk, sets a croissant into the oven, and once everyone has left it begins a laundry cycle and folds the dried clothes into stacks.

interactions.title: The stage-fright robot
interactions.description: At its CES 2018 keynote debut the Hub Robot went silent three times in a row as an LG executive asked it about the laundry, dinner, and a chicken recipe, until he conceded on stage that "even robots have bad days."

interactions.title: A face that reacts when you talk to it
interactions.description: Because CLOiD answers through both voice and on-screen facial expressions, it could mirror a small acknowledging look — a nod or smile — when you hand it a task, so a chore feels like a request to a companion rather than a command to a speaker.

interactions.title: A patrol pass before bed
interactions.description: Reusing the GuideBot's pre-planned patrol routes and onboard cameras, a home unit could roll a quiet last loop of the house at night, checking that doors are shut and lights are off and flagging anything out of place.

interactions.title: Hand off the folded laundry
interactions.description: With seven-degree-of-freedom arms and five-fingered hands that already fold and stack clothes, CLOiD could carry a finished stack to the right bedroom and set it on the bed, closing the loop between washing and putting away.
